name: Yum China Holdings, Inc.
business: Yum China Holdings, Inc. is the largest restaurant company in China in terms of 2025 system sales, with $11.8 billion of revenues in 2025 and 18,101 restaurants as of year-end 2025. It operates and franchises KFC, Pizza Hut, and emerging brands such as Lavazza, Huang Ji Huang, Little Sheep and Taco Bell. The company sells food and beverages to consumers through its restaurants and also generates revenue from franchise fees and supply chain transactions with franchisees.
sellsTo: Consumers in China through dine-in, delivery, and takeout
Franchisees (franchise fees and supply chain revenues)

exposures.sentence: Our growing restaurant network consists of our flagship KFC and Pizza Hut brands, as well as emerging brands such as Lavazza, Huang Ji Huang, Little Sheep and Taco Bell.

catalysts.event: Increase cash dividend to $0.29 per share payable on March 25, 2026
catalysts.sentence: On February 4, 2026, the Board of Directors declared a 21% increase in the cash dividend, raising it to $0.29 per share, payable on March 25, 2026, to stockholders of record as of the close of business on March 4, 2026.

catalysts.event: Share repurchase agreements for approximately $460 million through open market transactions for the first half of 2026
catalysts.sentence: The Company has entered into share repurchase agreements in the U.S. and Hong Kong for an aggregate repurchase amount of approximately $460 million through open market transactions for the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Plan to return $1.5 billion to shareholders in 2026
catalysts.sentence: The Company plans to return $1.5 billion to shareholders in 2026, adding to both $1.5 billion it delivered to shareholders in 2025 and 2024 in share repurchases and dividends.

catalysts.event: Fiscal year 2026 capital expenditures range of $600 million to $700 million
catalysts.sentence: We currently expect our fiscal year 2026 capital expenditures to be in the range of approximately $600 million to $700 million.
